description: Flanged shaft coupling aligned with the Z-axis, featuring a bottom flange, central hub, top flange, center bore with a keyway, and six through-bolt holes on the flanges.
components: Bottom flange disc (Z=0 to Z=10, diameter 50mm)
Central hub cylinder (Z=10 to Z=50, diameter 28mm)
Top flange disc (Z=50 to Z=60, diameter 50mm)
Center bore (diameter 14mm, full length Z=0 to Z=60)
Rectangular keyway (width 5mm, depth 2.5mm on +X side, full length Z=0 to Z=60)
6x Bolt holes (diameter 6.5mm, on a 38mm PCD, full length Z=0 to Z=60)
notes: The prompt specifies the keyway is centered at X = (bore radius - half keyway depth) = 5.75mm. Note that a standard keyway cuts *into* the hub wall, which would require the center of the keyway box to be at X = (bore radius + half keyway depth) = 8.25mm (to cut from X=7.0 to X=9.5). Please implement the keyway such that it cuts 2.5mm deep into the bore wall on the +X side (extending to X=9.5mm). The bolt holes only cut the flanges as the hub radius (14mm) is smaller than the bolt circle inner boundary (19 - 3.25 = 15.75mm).
